heading: M10+ Tier Backup Options
body:
To enable Cloud Backup for an `M10+` Atlas cluster, set
the Turn on Cloud Backup toggle to On. When
enabled, Atlas takes snapshots of your databases at regular
intervals and retains them according to your project's [base snapshot schedule](cloud-provider-retention-policy).

To disable Cloud Backup for an `M10+` Atlas cluster, set the
Turn on Cloud Backup toggle to Off. This
also disables Continuous Cloud Backup for the cluster. To retain any existing
snapshots after Cloud Backup is disabled, set the Keep
existing snapshots after backups disabled? toggle to On
before you apply your changes to the cluster. This option instructs
Atlas to retain the existing snapshots and [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) for the
cluster according to your cluster's [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy).

> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy) enabled, you
> can't disable Cloud Backup. If the Backup Compliance Policy has the
> **Require Point in Time Restore to all clusters** option set
> to **On**, then you also can't disable Continuous Cloud Backup
> without assistance from MongoDB Support. To disable Continuous Cloud Backup,
> the security or legal representative specified for the Backup Compliance Policy must
> [MongoDB Support](request-support) and complete a verification
> process.

Atlas provides the following backup options for `M10+`
clusters:

| Backup Option           | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cloud Backup            | Atlas takes incremental snapshots of the data in your cluster and lets you restore the data from those snapshots. Atlas stores snapshots in the same cloud provider region as the replica set member targeted for snapshots. |
| Continuous Cloud Backup | After Atlas restores a snapshot, Atlas replays the oplog to restore a cluster from a particular point in time within a window specified in the backup policy.                                                                |
